turns.user_message: My chest has felt tight and heavy for the past few days, like a knot I just can’t untangle. It started out of nowhere on Tuesday, and now it’s just this constant, dull pressure that follows me everywhere. I keep trying to shake it off, but every time I think about how disconnected things have felt lately, it just tightens up again. I’m honestly just exhausted from pretending everything is fine when my body clearly knows something is off. It’s weird how quickly a simple shift in the air between us can make me feel so completely drained.

That heavy weight in my ribs hasn’t budged at all, and it’s actually getting harder to ignore as the days drag on. I catch myself holding my breath every time I walk into a room, bracing for that familiar coldness that’s been creeping into our interactions. It’s exhausting to constantly monitor my own reactions just to keep the peace, and my shoulders are permanently locked up from all the tension. I keep replaying every little exchange in my head, wondering when things got so quiet and stiff. I just want to breathe normally again without feeling like I’m walking on thin ice.

The pressure in my chest has turned into this sharp, aching tightness that makes it genuinely hard to take a full breath. I feel completely hollowed out, like every ounce of my energy is being siphoned away by this unspoken distance that’s just suffocating me now. My hands shake whenever I try to reach out, because I’m terrified of saying the wrong thing and making it all worse. I’m so tired of feeling like a stranger in my own space, constantly bracing for the next awkward silence. It’s like I’m drowning on dry land, and I don’t know how to pull myself back up.

generation_metadata.diversity_anchor: The speaker opens by describing a physical feeling. This just started recently.
